case_name: Heisler v. Advanced Dermatology of N.Y. P.C.

summary.brief: Heisler v. Advanced Dermatology of N.Y. P.C.. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff alleges that defendants failed to identify, biopsy, or remove a cancerous lesion on his neck and failed to supervise defendant Daniel Lebovits, a registered physician's assistant working at Advanced Dermatology of New York P.C.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Lebovits treated plaintiff's lesion over the course of about 15 visits without ever screening for skin cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff alleged that Lebovits represented himself as a doctor, and that defendants Richard W. Green, M.D. and Lawrence D. Jaeger, D.O. never examined plaintiff themselves.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Supreme Court providently exercised its discretion in denying plaintiff's motions to amend the complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The proposed General Business Law § 349 cause of action is barred by the three-year statute of limitations.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Even if plaintiff's fraud claims were not palpably insufficient, defendants would be prejudiced by the amendment of the complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Supreme Court providently denied plaintiff's motion to renew, as the deposition testimony by Dr. Green given after plaintiff moved for leave to amend, as well as certain progress note logs raised no new facts that would have changed the prior determination.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports a lesion on the neck treated over approximately 15 visits without screening for skin cancer.

focused_exam.skin: Lesion on neck, details not specified

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Lesion on neck - potential for malignancy

requestables.name: Dermatology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with dermatologist to evaluate neck lesion

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with neck lesion treated over multiple visits
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the neck lesion?

options.description: Biopsy the lesion to rule out malignancy
options.clinical_reasoning: Biopsy is necessary to confirm or rule out cancer
Monitoring without biopsy may delay diagnosis

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Standard practice to rule out malignancy

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to biopsy delayed diagnosis of potential cancer
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Biopsy to confirm or rule out cancer

patient_outcome.description: Lesion remained untreated for an extended period

key_findings: Failure to biopsy or remove lesion

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Biopsy could have led to earlier diagnosis

factual_timeline.event: Patient treated for neck lesion over 15 visits

allegations.allegation: Failure to identify, biopsy, or remove cancerous lesion
